resolution_basis: The road network is assembled from named road blocks and is explicitly represented as nodes and lane edges.
BIG samples a block and discards it when it intersects the extant network, retrying or backtracking; feasibility is therefore rejection-based.
evidence_locator: validity_strategy=PDF p. 3 Sec. III-B, Algorithm 1 lines 16-26

resolution_basis: The generated roads are circular arcs and parametric cubic connection roads; the centerline is a construction reference, not the stored representation.
The three-phase generator constructively builds the roundabout and its reported randomized procedure samples radius, incident points, and headings.
evidence_locator: generator_family=PDF pp. 2-3 Sec. III-A and PDF p. 4 Sec. III-B

resolution_basis: The phases choose circle and connection geometry to avoid overlap before output; they do not generate complete candidates and reject them after validation.
The TORCS phenotype and generated output are ordered straight and turn segments; control points are an indirect encoding, not a parametric-curve output.
The evolutionary algorithm synthesizes full tracks and explicitly applies polynomial mutation to the encoded track parameters.
The method generates full segment sequences and the genetic algorithm explicitly uses mutation as an evolution operator.

Validity is optimized by local-search and genetic fitness, with invalid configurations repaired through moves or disfavored by the fitness condition; no representation guarantees validity by construction.
PAIRED trains an adversary policy by reinforcement learning to place environment parameters, so it is both learned generation and environment design.
The adversary places the agent, goal, and obstacles to construct each environment; it does not select among a fixed task catalogue.
PAIRED rewards solvable, regret-producing environments during learning but the source does not report a post-generation validation procedure matching the controlled validity labels.
evidence_locator: validity_strategy=PDF p. 5 Sec. 4 and PDF p. 7 Sec. 5.1

resolution_basis: The method defines each scenario as ordered waypoint course-altering angles and inter-waypoint distances.
It constructs new scenarios by combinatorially permuting prepared navigation-element values; it does not replay or select pre-existing scenarios.
The paper states that supporting data are available only from the corresponding author upon reasonable request, not as an open asset release.
GenTe represents geometric terrain with height maps and combines those with physical terrain models and geometric primitives, yielding a hybrid terrain representation.
A VLM interprets prompts and calls parameterized terrain-construction functions, combining learned generation with constructive assembly.
The source reports human quality review and downstream policy evaluation, but no controlled validity strategy such as rejection, repair, or simulation validation of generated geometry.

resolution_basis: SLEDGE represents lanes as connected polyline lane graphs and encodes the scene in a rasterized latent map; it does not use the generic hybrid label.
